content.full_text:
July 20, 1977

MR. William D. Moriarty
West County Business & Professional Assn.
228 Carlisle Way
Benicia, California  94510

Dear Mr. Moriarty:

Thank you for your letter of March 4 in which you ask whether the
West County Business and Professional Association's political action
committee must file campaign statements when little or no committee
activity has taken place so far this year. A recipient committee
must only file off-year reports when it has raised or spent money
during the 6 month period or has not filed a complete campaign
statement in connection with an election during the period. If
there is no obligation to file either due to the lack of activity
or because reports have been filed in connection with an election,
a voluntary "Report of no Activity" may be filed. The off-year
reporting periods are January 1 through June 30 due July 31 and
July 1 to December 31 due January 31.

The Statement of Organization, Form 410, only need be amended if
there is a change in the information contained on the Statement.

Enclosed are some informational fact sheets which may be of some
assistance. Please let me know if I can be of further assistance.

Very truly yours,

Regina Siciliano-Kutchins
Technical Assistance Program
Coordinator

RSK:bw

Enclosures

State of California

Memorandum

To : Gina Kutchins
     Fair Political Practices Commission

Date: July 13, 1977

From : Secretary of State - Janet E. Sandlin, Auditor
        Political Reform Division

Subject: Attached Letter Dated March 4, 1977, from West County Business
         and Professional Association (William D. Moriarty)


Mr. Moriarty called today because he had received no reply to
his letter. Can you help him out?




Attachment

West County Business & Professional Association
c/o William D. Moriarty
228 Carlisle Way, Benicia,CA 94510

March 4, 1977


Fair Political Practices Committee
1100 K Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

RE: Committee No. 741962

Gentlemen:

During a recent conversation with Mrs. Janet Sandlin of your office,
it was suggested that we explain our financial activity for 1976 and
verify our understanding that we need not file Committee Campaign State-
ment (Form 420) until our financial activity changes. It is our under-
standing that should our expenditures in behalf of California candi-
dates exceed $500 or receipts identifiable for political purposes
exceed $500 in any calendar year, resumption of filing would be required.

The WCBPA is a group of business and professional people residing
or working in the west portion of Contra Costa County. As part of our
interests, we have donated to political candidates and have had fund
raising activities to support our interests.

Our last Committee Campaign Statement covered periods through
December 31, 1975.

For 1976, excluding activities related to federal office candidates,
our income was from membership dues in the amount of $705. Expenses of
running the organization, covering meeting luncheons and secretarial
expenses, was $670.59, leaving only $32.41 that could have been applied
to political contributions. Contributions made to California candidates
were $400, divided between three candidates.

Since both of these figures are less than $500, we expect that filing
of Committee Campaign Statements for 1976 and for the future (until either
figure is over $500) will not be required.

May we have your concurrence in our interpretations of the guide-
lines?

Should we continue updating our Committee Statement of Organization,
as we have enclosed, even though Campaign Statements might not be required?

Thank you for your assistance. If a phone call would help clarify
our situation, please feel free to call me at (415) 235-9300.

Very truly yours,

[signature]

William D. Moriarty
Treasurer

sections.question: Must the West County Business and Professional Association's political action committee file campaign statements when little or no committee activity has taken place so far this year? Does the Statement of Organization, Form 410, need to be amended if there is no change in the information contained on the Statement?
sections.conclusion: A recipient committee must only file off-year reports when it has raised or spent money during the 6 month period or has not filed a complete campaign statement in connection with an election during the period. If there is no obligation to file either due to the lack of activity or because reports have been filed in connection with an election, a voluntary 'Report of no Activity' may be filed. The off-year reporting periods are January 1 through June 30 due July 31 and July 1 to December 31 due January 31. The Statement of Organization, Form 410, only need be amended if there is a change in the information contained on the Statement.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a recipient committee with minimal activity must file off-year campaign statements, and whether Form 410 (Statement of Organization) requires updates when organizational information has not changed.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Recipient committees need only file off-year reports if they have raised or spent money during the reporting period, or have not filed reports in connection with an election; they may file a voluntary 'Report of no Activity' if no filing obligation exists. Form 410 amendments are only required if the organization's information actually changes.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request (attached memorandum and original letter from Moriarty dated March 4, 1977) and the FPPC response letter dated July 20, 1977. Response clearly addresses the questions posed regarding filing requirements and Form 410 amendment requirements. The incoming request provides helpful context about the association's specific financial activity ($705 income, $672.59 expenses, $400 in political contributions for 1976).

CONCLUSION: A recipient committee must only file off-year reports when it has raised or spent money during the 6 month period or has not filed a complete campaign statement in connection with an election during the period. If there is no obligation to file either due to the lack of activity or because reports have been filed in connection with an election, a voluntary 'Report of no Activity' may be filed. The off-year reporting periods are January 1 through June 30 due July 31 and July 1 to December 31 due January 31. The Statement of Organization, Form 410, only need be amended if there is a change in the information contained on the Statement.

embedding.summary: FPPC responds to West County Business and Professional Association's inquiry regarding filing obligations for their political action committee given minimal financial activity in 1976, confirming that off-year reports are only required if money was raised or spent, and that Form 410 need only be updated if organizational information changes.
